action.title: HyperPBI showcase
action.message: 84 components, 12 categories, one compact CSV, and one HyperPBI 2.0 configuration.

pageHeader.title: HyperPBI Component Showcase
pageHeader.subtitle: Minimal, practical, and advanced usage backed by one compact synthetic dataset

footer.secondaryText: Uses exact Power BI runtime field keys from the included Field Manifest.

standardInfo.text: This component was created from a reusable root definition.

children.title: HyperPBI Component Showcase
children.text: This compact bundle demonstrates **84 component types across 12 categories**, plus datasets, calculations, definitions, application patterns, interactions, the application shell, and the Studio authoring workflow. Use the left sidebar to open any component page.

children.title: Data and logical datasets
children.text: One CSV feeds all pages. Named datasets demonstrate filter, derive, select, groupBy, metrics, sort, limit, static schema propagation, and row lineage.

children.title: Derived fields and sorted active rows
Calculations DSL
children.text: Root metrics are safe JSON aggregates. Dataset `derive` creates row-level fields inside a named dataset. No JavaScript, DAX text, SQL, or functions are executed.

children.title: Reusable definitions
children.text: Definitions deep-merge objects, replace arrays, remove definition IDs, and require each instance to supply its own stable ID.

children.title: Application patterns
children.text: Patterns are authoring conveniences that expand to ordinary runtime components with deterministic child IDs.

children.title: Interactions and UI actions
children.text: Controls filter, data points highlight or select, and safe UI actions manage tabs, overlays, toasts, scrolling, sidebar state, and filter clearing.

uiAction.title: Interactions
uiAction.message: UI actions are declarative and allowlisted.

children.title: Studio and AI-assisted workflow
children.text: 1. Bind fields and review the Field Manifest. 2. Generate a prompt for an approved external AI. 3. Paste one JSON object back into Studio. 4. Validate, preview, repair diagnostics if needed, and save. HyperPBI does not call an AI service or store an AI key.

items.label: Read diagnostics
items.description: Use exact path, code, and message.

items.label: Preview
items.description: The last valid saved dashboard remains protected.

children.title: Security boundary
children.text: No user JavaScript, eval, callbacks, inline handlers, arbitrary SQL, credentials, or unbounded network datasets are allowed.
